rationale: This structure is designed to mirror the major communities and intellectual divisions recognized by working physicists, closely aligning with the primary arXiv categories and APS divisions. We chose to split the vast area of 'Astrophysics' from 'Cosmology & Gravitation' to distinguish between the study of celestial objects and the study of the universe's overall structure and origin. Similarly, the traditional 'AMO' field was split into 'Atomic & Molecular Physics' and 'Optics & Photonics' to reflect their distinct research communities and applications. Condensed Matter Physics remains a single, large category focused on fundamental phenomena, with applied materials work explicitly excluded. A 'Statistical & Nonlinear Physics' category was created to group diverse but related fields like complex systems, chaos, and fluid dynamics.

subspecialties.name: Condensed Matter Physics
subspecialties.tagline: Quantum mechanics of solids and liquids, from superconductors to topological materials.
covers: Superconductivity, magnetism, semiconductors, quantum Hall effect, soft matter physics
explicitly_excludes: Materials synthesis and characterization (→ Materials Science)

subspecialties.name: Astrophysics
subspecialties.tagline: The physics of stars, galaxies, exoplanets, and the interstellar medium.
covers: Stellar evolution, galaxy formation, black holes, exoplanet detection, gravitational waves
explicitly_excludes: Early universe and large-scale structure (→ Cosmology & Gravitation)

subspecialties.name: Particle Physics
subspecialties.tagline: Probing the fundamental particles and forces at the highest energies.
covers: Standard Model physics, Higgs boson, neutrinos, supersymmetry, string theory, astroparticle physics
explicitly_excludes: Nuclear structure and reactions (→ Nuclear Physics)

subspecialties.name: Quantum Information & Computing
subspecialties.tagline: Harnessing quantum phenomena for computation, communication, and sensing.
covers: Qubits, quantum algorithms, quantum cryptography, error correction, quantum sensors
explicitly_excludes: Foundational quantum mechanics (→ Atomic & Molecular Physics)

subspecialties.name: Cosmology & Gravitation
subspecialties.tagline: The origin, evolution, and ultimate fate of the universe as a whole.
covers: General relativity, dark matter, dark energy, cosmic microwave background, inflation
explicitly_excludes: Physics of individual stars and galaxies (→ Astrophysics)

subspecialties.name: Optics & Photonics
subspecialties.tagline: The science of light, from lasers and imaging to quantum optics.
covers: Lasers, fiber optics, nonlinear optics, nanophotonics, quantum optics

subspecialties.name: Nuclear Physics
subspecialties.tagline: The structure, properties, and reactions of atomic nuclei.
covers: Nuclear structure, radioactive decay, nuclear reactions, quark-gluon plasma, nuclear astrophysics

subspecialties.name: Atomic & Molecular Physics
subspecialties.tagline: The quantum world of atoms, molecules, and their interactions with light.
covers: Atomic clocks, ultracold atoms, Bose-Einstein condensates, molecular spectroscopy
explicitly_excludes: Quantum computing hardware (→ Quantum Information & Computing)

subspecialties.name: Statistical & Nonlinear Physics
subspecialties.tagline: The physics of complex systems, from chaos to collective behavior.
covers: Statistical mechanics, chaos theory, fluid dynamics, pattern formation, networks
explicitly_excludes: Physics of biological systems (→ Biophysics & Medical Physics)

subspecialties.name: Biophysics & Medical Physics
subspecialties.tagline: Applying physics principles to understand biology and improve medicine.
covers: Molecular biophysics, cellular mechanics, medical imaging (MRI, PET), radiation therapy
explicitly_excludes: General complex systems theory (→ Statistical & Nonlinear Physics)

subspecialties.name: Plasma Physics
subspecialties.tagline: The study of the fourth state of matter for fusion, space, and industry.
covers: Fusion energy, space plasmas, industrial plasmas, plasma propulsion

subspecialties.name: Geophysics
subspecialties.tagline: The physics of the Earth, from its deep core to its magnetic field.
covers: Seismology, geomagnetism, mantle convection, rock physics, climate dynamics
explicitly_excludes: Planetary science of other worlds (→ Astrophysics)

subspecialties.name: Mathematical Physics
subspecialties.tagline: Developing rigorous mathematical frameworks for physical theories.
covers: Integrable systems, quantum field theory methods, general relativity mathematics, random matrix theory

uncovered_concerns: Computational Physics was not made a separate category, as it is a methodology that permeates all other subspecialties rather than a distinct research area.
Fluid Dynamics was merged into 'Statistical & Nonlinear Physics' as a standalone category felt too narrow for a physics-focused audience and has significant overlap.
Astroparticle physics is a major overlap area; we have placed it primarily under Particle Physics, though many papers could also fit in Astrophysics or Cosmology.
